name: Obón, Alcaine y Embalse de Cueva Foradada — circular desde Hoz de la Vieja
region_intro: Mountain biking around La Hoz De La Vieja features a landscape characterized by varied terrain, including dry riverbeds, canyons, and rolling hills. The region offers significant elevation changes, providing challenging climbs and rewarding descents for mountain bikers. Its geology often includes rocky paths and open, arid stretches, interspersed with areas of scrubland and occasional pine forests. This diverse environment makes for engaging mountain bike trails with a mix of technical sections and faster-flowing paths. Best mountain bike trails around La Hoz De La Vieja The most popular mountain bike trail is Vuelta al Barranco Ancho, a 39.1 miles (62.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 44 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain and varied surfaces. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra de Sant Just Loop Trail, a difficult 36.5 miles (58.8 km) path. This loop offers extensive views and demanding sections through the Sierra de Sant Just. Local mountain bikers also love the Muniesa - Huesa del Común -, a 38.5 miles (61.9 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and open country, often completed in about 5 hours 38 minutes. Mountain biking around La Hoz De La Vieja is defined by challenging climbs, technical descents, and scenic routes through canyons and hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult mountain bike trails. The routes in La Hoz De La Vieja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from a few reviews. Dozens of mountain bikers have used komoot to explore La Hoz De La Vieja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in La Hoz De La Vieja?
A: There are 13 mountain bike trails around La Hoz De La Vieja listed on komoot, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in La Hoz De La Vieja?
A: The mountain bike trails in La Hoz De La Vieja are predominantly challenging. Out of 13 routes, 7 are classified as difficult, 5 as moderate, and 1 as easy. This indicates a region well-suited for riders seeking demanding climbs and technical descents.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or beginner mountain bike routes in La Hoz De La Vieja?
A: While most routes are moderate to difficult, there is one easy mountain bike trail available. For families or beginners, it's recommended to carefully review the route details, elevation profiles, and surface conditions before embarking on a ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in La Hoz De La Vieja?
A: The region features varied terrain, including dry riverbeds, canyons, and rolling hills. You'll encounter rocky paths and open, arid stretches, interspersed with scrubland and occasional pine forests. Expect significant elevation changes, providing both challenging climbs and rewarding descents.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in La Hoz De La Vieja?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996262168/obon-alcaine-y-embalse-de-cueva-foradada-circular-desde-hoz-de-la-vieja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obón, Alcaine y Embalse de Cueva Foradada — circular desde Hoz de la Vieja&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 72.3 km loop. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006804653/maicas-cortes-de-aragon-and-la-hoz-de-la-vieja-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maicas, Cortes de Aragón, and La Hoz de la Vieja Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 45.3 km.

Q: What are some of the longer mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Several trails offer substantial distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996262168/obon-alcaine-y-embalse-de-cueva-foradada-circular-desde-hoz-de-la-vieja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obón, Alcaine y Embalse de Cueva Foradada — circular desde Hoz de la Vieja&lt;/a&gt; is the longest at 72.3 km. Other long routes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006865421/vuelta-al-barranco-ancho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Barranco Ancho&lt;/a&gt; (62.9 km) and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006847693/muniesa-huesa-del-comun" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muniesa - Huesa del Común -&lt;/a&gt; (61.9 km).

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in La Hoz De La Vieja?
A: The region offers several points of interest. You can explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5377977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portal de Santa Engracia&lt;/a&gt;, or visit charming settlements such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8027234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montalbán&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3694497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroyas&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5143126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obón&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5143010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz de la Vieja&lt;/a&gt;. These attractions can be integrated into your rides for cultural and scenic breaks.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in La Hoz De La Vieja?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the challenging climbs, technical descents, and scenic routes through canyons and hills, highlighting the diverse and engaging environment for mountain biking.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in La Hoz De La Vieja?
A: Given the arid characteristics of the region, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for mountain biking. Temperatures are milder, and the risk of extreme heat or winter conditions is lower, making for more comfortable rides. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures, though heavy snow is less common.

Q: Are there any specific trails that offer extensive views?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005500651/sierra-de-sant-just-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Sant Just Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is known for offering extensive views, particularly through the demanding sections of the Sierra de Sant Just. This difficult 58.8 km route provides panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in La Hoz De La Vieja?
A: Specific parking information varies by trailhead. For most routes, you can typically find parking in or near the starting villages or designated trail access points. It's advisable to check the individual route descriptions on komoot for detailed parking suggestions for your chosen trail.
tips: We are in a beautiful Teruel town located in the Cuencas Mineras region with about 81 inhabitants in 2020. Do you dare to discover the reason for the town's name? It is a curious story.

Obón is a beautiful town, a Spanish municipality in the province of Teruel that is located in the region of the Cuencas Mineras, in the Iberian System and in the valley of the Martín river. It is located to the south of the Cueva Foradada reservoir and highlights the tourist attraction of the Martín River Cultural Park that will not leave us indifferent.

Stretching from Alcaine to Oliete in the Sierra de Arcos, the Cueva Foradada reservoir stores the water of the Martín River, and belongs to the Ebro Hydrographic Confederation. From this point we have magnificent views of the reservoir from the road.
